summary: 19th-century Russian Realist painter and most prominent member of The Wanderers (Peredvizhniki). Known for Barge Haulers on the Volga, Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks, Ivan the Terrible and His Son Ivan, and Religious Procession in Kursk Province.

works.name: Barge Haulers on the Volga (1870-1873)
works.description: Eleven laborers struggling to pull a boat up a river. Clement Greenberg contrasted this with Picasso in 'Avant-Garde and Kitsch.' Wikipedia

clues.clue: Eleven laborers/men wearing straps pulling a boat up the Volga River
examples: showed eleven laborers struggling to pull a boat up a river
depicted men wearing straps as they walk along the title river

clues.clue: Clement Greenberg contrasted this with Picasso in 'Avant-Garde and Kitsch' — peasant would prefer the kitsch of this Russian realist
examples: Clement Greenberg contrasted Picasso with a realist artist from this country who painted a desolate landscape traversed by barge haulers
Clement Greenberg's essay suggests a peasant would prefer the kitsch works of this Russian realist over the avant-garde Picasso

works.name: Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks (1880-1891)
works.description: Mustached men surrounding a large table, laughing as they compose an insulting letter to Ottoman Sultan Mehmed IV. Received record-breaking payment. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Mustached men at a table laughing as they write an insulting letter to Ottoman Sultan Mehmed IV; fat man in white fur cap and red clothes
examples: this member of the Wanderers painted mustached men surrounding a large table where a letter to the Turkish Sultan is being written
A fat man in a white fur cap and red clothes laughs in another of this man's paintings

clues.clue: Letter asserts 'the Devil shits and your army swallow it'; insults sultan as 'a Macedonian mechanic' and 'a nasty glob of spit'
examples: composing a text which asserts that 'the Devil shits and your army swallow it'
received a record-breaking payment for an 1891 painting depicting the title Zaporozhian Cossacks

works.name: Ivan the Terrible and His Son Ivan (1885)
works.description: Crazed monarch clutching his dying son, blood on carpet and hands. Vandalized in 2018 by Igor Podporin with a metal pole. Wikipedia

images.caption: Ivan the Terrible and His Son Ivan

clues.clue: Maddened tsar/ruler holds his bloodied son on a red carpet; cane/staff lies on the floor; wide-eyed, trying to cover wound on son's head
examples: In 2018, a man with a metal pole vandalized a painting by this artist in which a cane lies on a blood red carpet below a ruler holding his bloodied son in his arms
this man wide-eyed as he cradles the body of his son

clues.clue: Vandalized in 2018 by Igor Podporin with a metal pole because he believed it was a 'lie'
examples: In May 2018, a man supposedly concerned with historical inaccuracy destroyed this man's painting of Ivan the Terrible and His Son Ivan with a metal pole
Igor Podporin smashed the glass on Repin's portrait of this 16th century Russian tsar cradling his dead son

works.name: Religious Procession in Kursk Province (1880-1883)
works.description: Crowd of Orthodox priests transporting an icon of the Virgin Mary; bearded man attempts to strike a blond boy with a stick; hunchbacked boy prevented from joining. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Crowd of Orthodox priests transporting an icon of the Virgin Mary in golden shrine; bearded man with stick tries to strike a blond boy; hunchbacked boy tries to join
examples: bearded man attempts to strike a blond boy with a stick on the left-hand side

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Most prominent member of The Wanderers (Peredvizhniki). Other works: They Did Not Expect Him, Sadko in the Underwater Kingdom, Dragonfly, What Freedom!, Duchess Sophia, Saint Nicholas Saves Three Innocents. Painted Tretyakov, surgeon Evgeny Pavlov. Studied under Maria Fortuny.

comprehensive_summary:
Ilya Repin was the most prominent 19th-century Russian Realist painter and leader of The Wanderers (Peredvizhniki). He is most commonly identified by Barge Haulers on the Volga and Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks.

Barge Haulers on the Volga depicts eleven laborers struggling to pull a boat up the Volga River. Clement Greenberg famously contrasted this work with Picasso in 'Avant-Garde and Kitsch,' suggesting a peasant would prefer Repin's kitsch over Picasso's avant-garde.

Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks shows mustached men at a table laughing uproariously as they compose an insulting letter to Ottoman Sultan Mehmed IV. The letter insults the sultan as 'a Macedonian mechanic' and 'a nasty glob of spit.' It received a record-breaking payment in 1891.

Ivan the Terrible and His Son Ivan depicts a crazed monarch clutching his dying son, with blood on a red carpet and a cane on the floor. It was vandalized in 2018 by Igor Podporin with a metal pole because he believed it was historically inaccurate.

Religious Procession in Kursk Province shows Orthodox priests carrying a gilded icon of the Virgin Mary amid a large crowd, with a bearded man trying to strike a blond boy with a stick and a hunchbacked boy prevented from joining.

Other works include They Did Not Expect Him (a woman seeing her gaunt son return), Sadko in the Underwater Kingdom (mermaid procession), Dragonfly (his daughter on a branch), What Freedom!, and Saint Nicholas Saves Three Innocents. He painted portraits of the art collector Pavel Tretyakov and surgeon Evgeny Pavlov. He studied under Maria Fortuny and earned a cult following in the Soviet Union.
